articles.title: Can Shree Cement, Ambuja's capex breather allay sector’s overcapacity concerns?
articles.summary: Pan-India average cement prices improved by ₹10-13 per bag month-on-month in May as companies attempted price hikes to partially offset the cost inflation arising due to the West Asia crisis
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Small-cap stock under ₹100 MIC Electronics zooms 9% despite weak trends on Dalal Street
articles.summary: MIC Electronics share price has fallen 20% this year so far, and has declined over 42% in six months. The smallcap stock has dropped 33% in one year, and has plunged 22% in two years. MIC Electronics shares have delivered multibagger returns of 167% in three years.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Hero Motocorp vs Bajaj Auto: Which is a better stock to buy after Q4 results 2026?
articles.summary: Q4 results 2026: Experts believe, both Bajaj Auto and Hero MotoCorp delivered strong Q4 results, but Bajaj Auto appears relatively better positioned for superior long-term wealth creation
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Multibagger defence stock Apollo Micro Systems jumps 7% after this order book update
articles.summary: Apollo Micro Systems shares rose over 7% on May 8 after announcing new orders totaling ₹51.02 crore. The contracts include ₹17.47 crore from the Ministry of Defence and others from public and private sectors, enhancing investor confidence in the defence and aerospace market.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: SBI Q4 Results 2026 LIVE: Net profit may rise 4% despite treasury pain, NII seen up 8% YoY; dividend in focus
articles.summary: SBI Q4 Results 2026 LIVE: SBI is expected to report muted earnings growth in the fourth quarter of FY26, weighed down by treasury losses. Margins and asset quality of the PSU bank is expected remain largely stable.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Nvidia is now worth more than India’s entire equity market
articles.summary: Nvidia's market cap has surged to $5.05 trillion, surpassing India's entire equity market. Its transformation into a key player in the AI industry reflects Wall Street's growth narrative, while Indian markets face challenges from foreign investors favouring AI-focused regions.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Rajesh Palviya of Axis Sec suggests NBCC, Lupin, RBL Bank shares to buy today - 8 May
articles.summary: The stock market opened lower on May 8 as crude oil prices rose and US-Iran tensions escalated. Nifty 50 and Sensex declined, but midcaps performed better. Analysts see a bullish trend in certain sectors, suggesting potential upside for investors.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Dabur India share price jumps 4% after Q4 results. Should you buy, sell or hold the FMCG stock?
articles.summary: Dabur India share price has fallen 18% in one month and has declined 20% in three months. The stock has dropped 15% in one year and has plunged 24% over the past three years.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: OnEMI Technology Solutions share price makes a strong debut, opens 11% higher at ₹190 apiece on NSE
articles.summary: OnEMI Technology Solutions shares opened at ₹190, rising 11.11% above the issue price. The IPO was heavily subscribed, receiving 9.50 times bids, and raised ₹926 crore, highlighting strong institutional demand.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Gold Rises as Buying Interest Counters Concern Over Hormuz Clash
articles.summary: Gold rose as signs of buying interest emerged in the wake of strong purchases by China’s central bank, even as fresh clashes in the Middle East threatened to fracture a fragile ceasefire.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Lenskart share price slips 3% as IPO lock-in period ends, block deal buzz
articles.summary: Lenskart shares dropped nearly 2% on May 8 as its six-month shareholder lock-in expired, making 1,047.4 million shares eligible for trading. A report indicated existing investors may exit through a block deal involving up to 70.2 million shares valued at $350 million.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Markets Debate If Japan Sold Treasuries When Intervening in Yen
articles.summary: The Federal Reserve’s custody holdings of Treasuries fell for the first time in a month at a time Japan was likely intervening to support its currency, with markets debating whether the nation offloaded US securities to fund its yen purchases.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Gold price today: Rate up on MCX amid healthy spot demand, positive global cues; experts highlight key levels to watch
articles.summary: Gold prices have been under pressure since the US-Iran war started on 28 February this year. Domestic spot gold prices, as per MCX, have declined 5% since then, pressured by higher oil prices.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Rupee opens 33 paise lower at 94.58 against the US dollar
articles.summary: The rupee opened at 94.58 against the US dollar, impacted by escalating US-Iran tensions and rising crude oil prices. Analysts indicate that a sustainable recovery for the rupee hinges on stabilizing oil prices and reduced dollar demand from oil refiners.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Oil prices rise after US, Iran exchange fire at Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Oil prices rose after US and Iranian forces exchanged fire at the Strait of Hormuz, the first clash since the 7 April ceasefire, stoking fresh supply fears in a key global shipping lane.

articles.title: After BJP victory, Centre begins review of pending schemes in Bengal
articles.summary: A fractious relationship between the state and Centre—run by the Trinamool Congress TMC and the BJP-led National Democratic Alliance, respectively—meant several central schemes became mired in disputes.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Parliamentary panel to review domestic output of petroleum products on 15 May
articles.summary: India’s parliamentary panel will review fuel and fertilizer supplies on 15 May amid disruptions caused by the West Asia war. While petrol and diesel stocks remain stable, LPG shortages persist due to the Strait of Hormuz blockade, prompting booking restrictions and supply monitoring.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Broad-based sector stress drove ECLGS 5.0 rollout, says finance secretary
articles.summary: On Wednesday, the government approved the scheme with a total outlay of ₹2.55 trillion, including a dedicated ₹5,000 crore window for airlines. The scheme allows additional credit of up to 20% of peak working capital utilised for eligible borrowers affected by the conflict.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Oil prices steady as US, Iran consider fresh proposal for peace deal
articles.summary: Oil prices steadied after an 8% drop as hopes of a US-Iran peace deal lifted sentiment, though conflicting signals from Donald Trump capped gains.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Sebi’s policy u-turns raise questions on consultations, regulatory certainty
articles.summary: Sebi's recent policy changes have sparked concerns over regulatory uncertainty, with four decisions reversed after stakeholder feedback. Critics emphasize the importance of consistent consultation, which was missing in some two instances.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Welfare spending surge pushes subsidy outgo to 91% of target
articles.summary: Food subsidy, the largest component, stood at ₹1.92 trillion, or 85% of the revised estimate of ₹2.28 trillion. However, the urea subsidy has already breached its annual target, reaching ₹1.28 trillion, or 101% of the revised estimates, and higher than the ₹1.16 trillion recorded a year ago.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: How India’s growth story has left its workers behind
articles.summary: Noida’s unrest highlights India’s structural crisis: skipping manufacturing for services has left a low-skilled workforce with stagnant real wages, poor living and working conditions, and diminishing bargaining power.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India’s services sector recovers lost ground, domestic market fuels growth in April: HSBC India Services PMI
articles.summary: India's services sector showed recovery in April, with the HSBC PMI rising to 58.8. Increased output and new business orders were noted, alongside a shift to domestic suppliers. The composite PMI also rose to 58.2, indicating growth momentum across sectors.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: US Envoy Sergio Gor confirms Marco Rubio will visit India soon to ‘build even stronger ties’
articles.summary: US envoy Sergio Gor announced that Indian companies plan to invest over $20.5 billion in various sectors, contributing to job creation and stronger supply chains. The SelectUSA Investment Summit promotes foreign direct investment, connecting global business leaders with US economic developers.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: West Asia war may briefly dent India's growth as fundamentals strong: Memani
articles.summary: CII President Rajiv Memani, who is also the regional managing partner of EY Africa-India and chair of EY Growth Markets Council, said that the challenge before businesses is to strike a balance between the costs that they can absorb and those that must be passed on to consumers.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Govt clears ₹50,000-crore package to boost credit flow and support businesses
articles.summary: The government-backed loan support scheme will facilitate an additional credit flow of up to ₹2.55 trillion to the economy, including ₹5,000 crore specifically for the aviation sector, a government statement said.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: SCBs clock 15.9% credit growth in FY26, led by services, MSMEs, retail
articles.summary: The 497-basis-point jump reflects sustained economic momentum and rising demand for credit across sectors.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India plans to award construction-ready highway contracts, with all approvals in place, to curb delays
articles.summary: In a bid to eliminate persistent highway project delays, MoRTH is proposing stricter pre-award filters, ensuring all critical land and structural clearances are in place before contracts are awarded. This shift aims to make highway projects more execution-ready.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Why RBI wants to keep India's gold at home
articles.summary: RBI repatriated over 100 tonnes of gold to India in the six months to March, taking the total gold reserves stored locally to 680 tonnes. Why is the central bank bringing gold back home?
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: India among most resilient as global shocks test emerging markets: Moody’s Ratings
articles.summary: India and Thailand are well placed to manage future shocks because monetary policy frameworks are clear and predictable, inflation expectations are well anchored, and exchange rates can adjust when needed, Moody’s Ratings said.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: India’s fertilizer subsidy reaches ₹2.17 trillion in FY26 due to West Asia war, may rise by a fifth in FY27
articles.summary: As the war in West Asia disrupts supply chains, India's fertilizer subsidy may exceed ₹2.17 trillion in FY27. This trend raises concerns about fiscal discipline and food inflation.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: RBI reshuffles top deck: New DG Rohit Jain takes charge, portfolios realigned
articles.summary: Rohit Jain has been appointed deputy governor of the Reserve Bank of India, leading to a portfolio reshuffle among governors. The changes, effective 4 May, aim to enhance oversight of fintech and adapt to evolving financial priorities amid global volatility.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Centre plans 3.25 mn ha shift to chemical-free farming by FY31
articles.summary: The department of expenditure in coordination with the agriculture ministry, is drafting a proposal to create 65,000 clusters of 50 hectares each under the National Mission on Natural Farming
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Indian private sector new order growth quickens in April but remains slowest in 4 years: PMI survey shows
articles.summary: After losing some momentum in March due to West Asia conflict, growth across India's private sector expanded at a quicker rate at the start of new fiscal year in April.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Oil prices subdued after Trump says US plans to help stranded ships cross Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Oil prices held near $108 as Trump announced ‘Project Freedom’ to guide stranded ships through the Strait of Hormuz, though blockade risks and fragile peace prospects kept markets on edge.

articles.title: Rural job scheme demand dips by over a third in April—here’s why
articles.summary: The decline in rural work demand in April may be a continuation of similar declines seen over the past few months, though it could also reflect implementation challenges with the VB G-RAM-G.

articles.title: Oil slips after Trump says US will help free ships stranded in Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Oil prices fell on Monday after President Trump announced efforts to free stranded ships in the Strait of Hormuz, yet remained above $100 due to ongoing U.S.-Iran tensions. Brent crude dropped to $107.53, while U.S. West Texas Intermediate fell to $101.10 amid stalled peace negotiations.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Govt says auto fuel shortage rumour led to panic buys, assures stocks adequate
articles.summary: While cooking gas supplies remained strained due to the West Asia war, household demand had been being prioritized and no shortages have been reported, the petroleum ministry said.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: OPEC members increase oil production quota by 188,000 bpd, no word on UAE's withdrawal amid war in West Asia
articles.summary: Saudi Arabia, Russia, and five other OPEC members increased their oil production quota by 188,000 barrels per day, despite the UAE's recent withdrawal. Analysts suggest this move aims to project stability amid ongoing market disruptions from the Mideast conflict.
articles.label: BEARISH
